One million cubic yards Of dirt
will be moved by the Flick Con.
struction Co., holding the contract
Of filling in the Milwaukee trestles
| between Ellensburg and the Column
| bia river. Ten bridges will be re-
placed by sold earth fills.
Superintendent John M. RapeIje,
formerly of the Rocky Mountain
division of the Northern Pacific, ar.
rived in Spokane yesterday and is
registered at the Victoria. Mr.. Rap
ellie is to succeed Alfred Beamer as
superintendent Of the Idaho divis-
! ion.


The Home Telephone Co. has
completed the work OF laying $4,000
feet OF cable. OF those, $4,000 feet
have been put underground in the
downtown streets, while $4,000 feet
of aerial cable have been installed
on Cannon Hill and in Brownies
addition.


The Trustee company ls soon to
reopen negotiations with the Con-
solidated Improvement Co. for the
Empire State building or the pres
ent Spokane club building.


Mrs. Clayton Miller will begin the
building of a new five story struc-
ture east of Washington street, and
extending through from Sprague to
First. Plans are being drawn up
by Architects Alfred Jones and ]. 7.
! Levesque.


The expenditure of the 10000
club in connection with the taking
of the census will amount to about
$4,000. Last December the club COM
municated with the United States
census department to increase the
city enumerators from SO to 6S. EX-
Mayor G.. Herbert Moore is presi-
dent of the club. The census COM
mittee consists of F. w. King, A. w.
Jones, G. C. Corbaley and Hugo E.
Oswald.


| The Idaho E. Western Railway Co.
has filed application with the state
land office for right of way in east
ern Spokane county.


A comedy of errors was enacted


in Justice G. w. Suckers court


yesterday morning, when Blanche
Mcdonald was arrested for operat
ing a resort. When asked to plead


it was brought out that she had


been in Jail for six weeks.


| Charles C. Cook, who is contest


ing the homestead entry Of Joe O


Lamott to the south half of the


southwest quarter of section 4


township 10 north, range 80 E. w


M, has won when 1t was proved


that Lamott failed to reside on thu


land 81 to make improvements re
CURED by law.


George Carlson, 10 year old boy
was sentenced to serve six month


in the county jail by Judge j. D


IIinkle of the superior court, yestel
day. Sentence was suspended, how
, ever, during good behavior. Carlson
f had found a check, which he In
r dorsed and placed ON deposit
i. thereby obtaining money unde
false pretenses.
